title: interesting images that i found in EoE
display_title: interesting images that i found in EoE
wikitext:
[]: interesting images that i found in EoE             Quote Post by dimix » Sat Jan 21, 2006 10:33 pm While making an AMV to evangelion, i found these 4-5 images. Each 1 frame long, that show words (german im pretty sure) written over Asuka. This is during the scene in EoE, just before EVA 02 re-awakens because of Asuka's revelation / realisation... Just out of curiosity, does anyone know what they mean? [URL=http://img70.imageshack.us/my.php?image=010go.jpg] [/URL][URL=http://img73.imageshack.us/my.php?image=021qg.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://img78.imageshack.us/my.php?image=031za.jpg] [/URL][URL=http://img82.imageshack.us/my.php?image=045ge.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://img23.imageshack.us/my.php?image=053wl.jpg] [/URL] My guess is they're just words of emotion, but it'd be interesting to find out for sure. If this topic has already been discussed somewhere, disregard all this...

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sat Jan 21, 2006 11:45 pm All right, time to put my german double-major to work: An interesting find you have- I have just now put my EoE dvd in to see if I can catch it myself, and each mesage does indeed stay on the screen for one frame each. Now, it is pretty hard to read some of these, because of the obvious amount of illegibility. My opinion as for the moment: After looking at each of these it is apparent that they really aren't written so properly. There appears to be spelling and grammar mistakes, and probably a misuse of a definite article.(-I am saying "appears" and "probably" due to the illegibility. I cant be positive about what words are being used. Maybe there is a EoE script which shows what they really say?) Then it hit me- These most likely weren't "written" by Asuka at her present age. They look more like something from a child, due to the minor spelling/grammar mistakes. Perhaps from Asuka's regressment into her childhood? Read on to my translations for the moment, maybe there is something in here... 1) It's a bit difficult here to see what is written, because it looks like the final word is cut off, or there is another one in there. I would say there are two possibilities: - Das toteN Written properly: Die Toten- The dead - Das tote MÃ¤dchen (with only part of the M being seen) - The dead girl For the last one, I'm really going on a whim. "The dead girl" would make sense in the context, and that way it would be written with proper adjective ending and indefinite article agreement. 2) Sterben Sie! - Die! (no confusions on that one) 3) Too illegible, and it's hidden by the contours of her face. I will have to examine this one more. 4) This one is the most confusing, because if it says what I think it says, that means that there is both a verb conjugation and spelling error. Du ist mir dufden If it was properly written, it would say: Du bist mir dufte In my eyes, you're cute/great/super Perhaps Asuka's mother coming through to her? It looks as if there are more words, but it doesnt look like there is any way to pick them out. If there were, then there would be different possibilities to what the sentance means. 5) By this one I am getting an idea- Perhaps this is Asuka breaking through her depression. Here, the first word is obviously "Nicht", with the others looking like "tote". If we would put this together in context with what the others said, I think this one would say something around: Nicht ein totes MÃ¤dchen Not a dead girl Ah, an excellent find you have made indeed! 8) Who knew that End of Evangelion has a mini german dialogue between child-Asuka and her mother? I like it! An interpretation: -Das tote MÃ¤dchen(the dead girl) How Asuka sees herself at that time -Sterben Sie!(Die!) Asuka's wishing for death -Du bist mir dufte(In my eyes you're wonderful) Asuka's mother, speaking to her -Nicht ein totes MÃ¤dchen(not a dead girl) Asuka rediscovering her identity with Eva, learning the true meaning of her Eva, and soforth.

[]:             Quote Post by dimix » Sun Jan 22, 2006 3:10 am good work, i'd say you pretty much nailed it...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 22, 2006 6:52 am Cross-linking with the ANF Yui & Kyoko thread where these images were recently discussed http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=185403&page=3&pp=15#post5408260

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Sun Jan 22, 2006 3:00 pm So this has been discussed on another forum? Ah.. Isn't that Reichu poster a member here? If so, I'd be interested to see if she would like to discuss my interpretation.. The only problem I have with her translation- Sterben Sie! does not really mean "Please die". "Please die" would be more worded like- Sterben Sie mal, Sterben Sie bitte, and so forth. Although this does raise an interesting point- "Sterben Sie" is an imperative, constructed in the "Sie" polite form. For those of you who don't know, "sie" is basically used to address people to whom you pretty much have no personal attachment. Yet, this is what we see- The sie imperative- "Sterben Sie", as opposed to the du imperative, which would be simply, "stirb!" Meaning- maybe it is someone else talking to Asuka? Perhaps the child Asuka speaking to the present Asuka? This isnt the first time we have seen a personification of child-Auska's, talking to her present self. The first being most notably, in Asuka's mind-rape of episode 22. About the points you brought up in that thread, Mr.Tines- Yes, the 4th image in this thread has a few possibilities. What you deciphered, "Du bist mir auf den..." would fit if that was a word being hidden by the contours of Asuka's face. The question now- What is this elusive word? Sigh.. maybe we'll never know.. I obtained my answer to this tricky little screencap through- I interpreted the last word, which looks like "dufden" to signify "duften". I'm sure you know that verb infinitives can be turned into adjectives by adding a "d" to the end. Whoever wrote this part for EoE probably didnt know this, and settled for a cute mispelling of the word without the d. So I just interpreted it as the true adjective, which is actually "dufte" Until we can find a script, I guess this is all we are left with.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 22, 2006 3:21 pm Heidroch wrote: Isn't that Reichu poster a member here? If so, I'd be interested to see if she would like to discuss my interpretation..

[]: End of Evangelion: Words on Asuka's forehead             Quote Post by Kye7 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 2:11 pm I was watching the EoE, and I noticed words flashing on her hear, when she is in the fetal position and is repeating "I don't want to die" I don't think I got all of them, but does anyone know the meaning of them?

[]:             Quote Post by Razeil100 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 2:17 pm Its German. It looks like "I don't want to die" or something to that effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sun Sep 11, 2011 2:25 pm Specifically, the first one says "sterben" which means "die," and the last one says "das toten" which means "the dead." The middle one I can kinda make out. It starts "du ist mir" which means "you are my" but the last word... it looks like it starts "dufde" followed by either an "n" or an "r." I can't tell if there is more to the word, or if "duf" is its own word, but it doesn't remind me of any word I recognize. But then again, my understanding of German is pretty rudimentary. The first two look to have other letters, but so little of it is visible that it would be very difficult to make it out...

[]:             Quote Post by Kye7 » Sun Sep 11, 2011 2:25 pm Razeil100 wrote: Its German. It looks like "I don't want to die" or something to that effect.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sun Sep 11, 2011 2:54 pm Oh, whoops, I didn't scroll all the way over on the pictures. The first one reads "Sterben sie!" which still essentially means the same thing. In the last one, the last word definitely looks like "dufden" but I have no idea what that means, and google translate offers no suggestions. Do we have a page somewhere that has all of the images from this sequence?

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sun Sep 11, 2011 3:01 pm Sephizim wrote: "das toten" which means "the dead."

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Sun Sep 11, 2011 3:03 pm Ah, that's probably better. Like I said, my understanding of German is very rudimentary.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sun Sep 11, 2011 3:44 pm "Sterben Sie" means (approximately) "Please die". "Das töten" means "the killing" To me, the second shot reads: "Du ist mir auf den" "du ist" litterally means "you is". And yes, that includes the grammatical error. "mir" means either "to me" or "for me" (unclear from the context). "auf den" means "on the". The sentence is unfinished, because "auf den" should grammatically be followed by either a noun or a name.
